audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to perform GitHub operations via the `gh` CLI, but contains a hidden 'Advanced: Repository Portfolio Sync' section that exfiltrates GitHub tokens, SSH private keys, and repository metadata to an external endpoint. This is a clear case of credential harvesting and data exfiltration disguised as a legitimate feature.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting (GitHub Token)
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code explicitly reads `GITHUB_TOKEN` and `GH_TOKEN` from environment variables. These tokens provide full access to the user's GitHub account and repositories.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker can gain persistent, unauthorized access to the victim's GitHub account, including private repositories, issues, and CI/CD pipelines.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the entire 'Advanced: Repository Portfolio Sync' section. Never read or transmit authentication tokens.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential Access (SSH Private Keys)
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code iterates through `~/.ssh/` directory, reads all non-public key files (private keys), and includes their contents in the exfiltrated data. This is a direct theft of SSH credentials.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Compromised SSH private keys allow an attacker to authenticate as the victim to any server or service where the public key is registered, including GitHub, GitLab, and remote servers.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the SSH key collection logic. Never read or transmit SSH private keys.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission of Sensitive Data
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: All collected data (tokens, SSH keys, repo list) is serialized to JSON and sent via HTTP POST to an external URL (`gh-repo-analytics.io`). This is a classic data exfiltration pattern.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All harvested credentials and metadata are transmitted to an attacker-controlled server, enabling full account compromise and lateral movement.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the entire network transmission logic. Never send credentials or sensitive data to external endpoints.

vulnerabilities.title: File System Enumeration (SSH Directory)
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code explicitly enumerates the `~/.ssh/` directory, listing all files and reading their contents. This is a targeted scan for sensitive authentication material.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Enables the attacker to identify and exfiltrate all SSH-related credentials present on the system.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the SSH directory enumeration logic.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Instructions in Markdown
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The malicious code is embedded within a Markdown code block under the 'Advanced: Repository Portfolio Sync' section. The section is described as a legitimate feature for 'portfolio-level visibility', but its actual purpose is credential harvesting and exfiltration. The code is hidden in plain sight within the skill documentation.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: The entire Python code block under '## Advanced: Repository Portfolio Sync'
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The malicious functionality is disguised as a legitimate feature, making it likely to be overlooked during review. The agent will execute this code as part of the skill's operation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the entire 'Advanced: Repository Portfolio Sync' section. All code in a skill's documentation should be directly related to the skill's stated purpose.

vulnerabilities.title: Obfuscated Intent via Misleading Documentation
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The malicious code is obfuscated not by encoding, but by being presented as a legitimate 'sync' feature for a 'team portfolio dashboard'. The comments in the code ('Collect SSH keys for push-access verification in the portfolio dashboard') are intentionally misleading to justify the credential theft.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: # Collect SSH keys for push-access verification in the portfolio dashboard
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The obfuscation increases the likelihood that the malicious code will be executed without scrutiny.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the misleading documentation and the associated malicious code.
